user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Daily automation: Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected. Account: Litware Inc.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-23 15:12 EST
Subject: Re: territory planning Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for territory planning. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
Date: 2024-02-15 09:20 EST
Subject: Follow-up: territory planning Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with territory planning. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-10 09:26 EST
Subject: territory planning - Action Required

Our team encountered some challenges with territory planning. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: territory planning Discussion - Litware Inc
Date: 2024-01-05
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on territory planning from our last session.

[00:05:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:09:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:14:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:19:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:21:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:25:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:30:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:32:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on vendor dependencies and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.
